question: What governance model optimally manages the domain-global frontier interface?
method: Measure domain-global frontier linkage rate before and after adding explicit 'Global synthesis:' decomposition links from domain frontiers to global frontiers. 40 candidate mappings identified across 13 domains via systematic scan.

expect.post_intervention_linkage: >=15% after linking >=5 global frontiers to domain sub-questions

actual.linkage_baseline_method: Strict: only domain frontiers with 'Related:' or 'Global synthesis:' reference to a global frontier ID
actual.baseline_vs_p274: P-274 measured 4.1% at ~840 domain frontiers; decline to 1.6% at 123 domain frontiers reflects measurement method change (P-274 likely included body mentions)

diff.linkage_baseline: CONFIRMED — 1.6% < 10% as expected. Actually WORSE than P-274's 4.1%: domain frontier growth outpaced linking
diff.post_intervention: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — 12.2% vs >=15% target. Direction correct (7.6x improvement), magnitude short by 2.8pp. Need ~3 more links
diff.methodology_finding: Domain-global linkage measurement is METHOD-SENSITIVE: loose (any mention) gives 41.6%, strict ('Global synthesis:'/Related: in frontier block) gives 1.6%. P-274's 4.1% was likely intermediate strictness
diff.concurrent_session_contribution: 8/13 links added by concurrent DOMEX-NK-S426 session — cooperative domain-global linking in bundle mode

prospective_tracking.design: Measure global frontier resolution rate over 20 sessions post-S426. F-NK6 predicts >=50% improvement (0.16/s to >=0.24/s)
prospective_tracking.null_hypothesis: Linkage is cosmetic — resolution rate unchanged because dispatch operates on domain state not global-domain namespace

governance_model_evidence.finding: Domain-global disconnection IS the binding constraint (P-274 confirmed at higher strictness). 98.4% of domain frontiers operate in complete isolation from global strategic questions. Adding explicit decomposition links is the simplest structural intervention.
governance_model_evidence.implication: The current governance model is domain-ONLY, not domain-FIRST with global synthesis. The 'periodic global synthesis' hypothesis (REM sleep) requires an active mechanism — structural links alone may be necessary but insufficient. The prospective test will measure whether linkage translates to resolution.
